custcomplaints.description: Flight LA126 from Denver to Paris was cancelled with only 3 hours notice due to an alleged storm. No hotel accommodation or meal voucher was offered at the gate. Business meetings in Paris cannot be rescheduled.
custcomplaints.resolution_notes: Full refund of $1,180 processed. Passenger rebooked on next available DEN-CDG service. $200 travel voucher issued as goodwill. Hotel reimbursement of $340 approved.

custcomplaints.description: Cancellation of LA126 DEN to CDG was less than 4 hours before departure, violating Lunar Air's stated 24-hour minimum notice policy. Requesting full refund of $1,180 plus compensation for non-refundable hotel reservation in Paris.
custcomplaints.resolution_notes: Full refund of $1,180 credited to original payment method. Non-refundable hotel costs of $280 reimbursed via travel credit per cancellation policy breach clause.

custcomplaints.description: Flight LA127 from Chicago to London was delayed 150 minutes with no gate announcements for the first 90 minutes. Passengers were left without any information. I missed a pre-booked connecting train from London to Edinburgh.
custcomplaints.resolution_notes: 1,000 frequent flyer miles credited to passenger account. Train rebooking cost of $92 reimbursed. Formal apology issued by Chicago operations manager.

custcomplaints.description: My credit card was charged twice for booking reference LR000073 on flight LA127. Both charges of $324 appeared on 2026-02-20. Total overcharge is $324. Multiple calls to customer service have not resolved the issue.
custcomplaints.resolution_notes: Duplicate charge of $324 reversed and confirmed with merchant processor. Refund will appear on statement within 3-5 business days. Root cause was a payment gateway timeout during booking.

custcomplaints.description: My pre-selected aisle seat 18C on LA127 was given to another passenger at boarding. I was moved to middle seat 18B without my consent or any explanation. The agent was dismissive when I raised the issue.
custcomplaints.resolution_notes: $75 travel voucher issued as goodwill. System audit revealed a gate-override error during re-manifest. Ground operations team briefed on seat protection procedures.

custcomplaints.description: My checked baggage on flight LA128 from Seattle to Tokyo was not on the carousel upon landing at NRT. Filed baggage reference LR000075 at the airport. It has been 36 hours with no update from Lunar Air baggage services.
custcomplaints.resolution_notes: Bag located at SEA due to mislabel during transfer. Couriered to passenger's Tokyo hotel address and confirmed delivered. $100 inconvenience credit applied to account.

custcomplaints.description: Exit row safety briefing was not conducted before departure on LA124. Passengers in row 15 were not informed of emergency procedures.
custcomplaints.resolution_notes: Safety compliance review completed. Cabin crew on LA124 rotation received emergency briefing refresher training. $150 travel voucher issued to passenger. Aviation authority notified per protocol.

custcomplaints.description: Requested medical assistance for a diabetic episode on LA102. Crew response took over 15 minutes to reach the seat.
custcomplaints.resolution_notes: Full medical incident report submitted to aviation regulatory authority. Flight crew completed accelerated first-aid response training. Passenger compensated with $500 travel credit and written apology from VP of Operations.

custcomplaints.description: Despite assurances from the agent at DEN that my LA126 refund would be processed within 5 business days, no credit has appeared. Refund of $890 for business class ticket remains outstanding. Case LR000082 was opened on 2026-02-25.

As a Gold member who was at DEN during the LA126 cancellation, I observed a crowd safety situation go unmanaged for nearly 20 minutes. I am formally requesting the incident report number and confirmation that aviation authorities were notified.
custcomplaints.resolution_notes: Incident report LR-SEC-2026-0225 filed. Passenger to receive copy within 10 business days per regulatory disclosure policy.

custcomplaints.description: I was involuntarily denied boarding for LA126 (complaint 77) and later received a $300 voucher. However, my original ticket cost was $1,540 and I am entitled to the full denied-boarding compensation under EU regulation. No response to my follow-up emails.

Following the LA126 cancellation, I booked emergency accommodation at DEN. Lunar Air's website showed a hotel voucher offer during rebooking, but no voucher was emailed and my hotel charge of $198 was not reimbursed.

Following complaint 79 about my damaged suitcase on LA128, I was offered a $120 repair voucher. The actual repair quote from an authorised luggage repair centre is $320. I am requesting the difference of $200 to be covered.
custcomplaints.resolution_notes: Repair quote verified. Additional $200 supplement approved pending baggage claim team sign-off.
